claims.statement: On this machine and one-million-cell numeric public-API writer workload, FastXLSX Streaming median elapsed time was 1583 ms versus OpenXLSX 0.4.1 workbook API 3180 ms, a 2.01x throughput ratio; median peak working set was 6.87109 MB versus 395.258 MB.

On this machine and one-million-cell mixed workload with 20 percent strings, FastXLSX Streaming median elapsed time was 1248 ms versus OpenXLSX 3292 ms, a 2.64x throughput ratio; median peak working set was 6.88672 MB versus 403.957 MB.

Numeric output sizes were 3129660 bytes for FastXLSX and 3128643 bytes for OpenXLSX; mixed output sizes were 3413774 and 3337140 bytes. OpenXLSX used document.save() defaults while FastXLSX used explicit DEFLATE level 6, so this is a scoped public-API workload comparison rather than an identical-backend microbenchmark.
The OpenXLSX unique-string workload did not complete within 300 seconds. The timeout is retained as exploratory evidence and no OpenXLSX completion time or peak-memory value is inferred.
